steps.title: Spot the identifying name
steps.content: The proper name Doleschallia bisaltide directly follows the noun “species” to identify which species is meant.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: When a noun is followed directly by an identifying name, no punctuation separates them—just as with “the city Paris” or “the scientist Newton.” Options B (semicolon), C (colon), and D (comma) all incorrectly insert punctuation between the noun and its identifying name. That matches choice A — A is correct.
